name: Oxygen (O)-limited minimal medium, 0.08% glucose (PMC5464527)
description: Oxygen (O)-limited minimal medium, 0.08% glucose — medium reported in PMC5464527 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC5464527 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: Tweeddale, Notley-McRobb & Ferenci 1998 (same lab, same glucose-limited chemostat system): 'Minimal medium A (MMA) (28) was the basal salts medium for both chemostat and batch cultures.' MMA (Miller, Experiments in Molecular Genetics, 1972) per litre 1x: K2HPO4 10.5 g, KH2PO4 4.5 g, (NH4)2SO4 1 g, sodium citrate.2H2O 0.5 g.
provenance.base_medium: Minimal medium A (MMA) of Miller 1972 (Experiments in Molecular Genetics) -- the Ferenci-lab basal salts medium; here the MMA-based chemostat medium of Schliep, Ryall & Ferenci 2012, supplemented with 0.08% glucose and run with the sparger airflow blocked (oxygen-limited).

sources_chased.what: PMC5464527 Methods (base medium = ref 76 Schliep 2012 + 0.08% glucose; O-limited protocol = ref 77 Manche 1999)
added: FeSO4 + sodium citrate present in non-Fe-limited media

sources_chased.what: ref 76 Schliep, Ryall & Ferenci 2012, Mol. Biosyst. (base chemostat minimal medium)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch doi.org/10.1039/c2mb05495g redirected to paywalled OUP; Methods not readable

sources_chased.what: ref 77 Manche, Notley-McRobb & Ferenci 1999, Genetics 153:5 (PMC1460742) -- aerobic vs O-limited glucose-limited chemostats
sources_chased.how: WebFetch PMC page returned abstract + refs only (Methods behind PDF)

sources_chased.what: Tweeddale, Notley-McRobb & Ferenci 1998, J. Bacteriol. 180:5109 (PMC107546) -- same lab, same glucose-limited chemostat system: identifies basal medium
added: basal salts medium = Minimal medium A (MMA), Miller 1972, ref 28

sources_chased.what: Miller 1972 Minimal Medium A composition (K2HPO4/KH2PO4/(NH4)2SO4/citrate + Mg/glucose/thiamine)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch (Rice Bennett Lab MinA wiki / iGEM HKUST-Rice recipe reproduce Miller MinA)

missing_before: ammonium sulfate 1.0 g/L (was only a generic NH4 exchange)
recuration.notes: O-limited = oxygen-depleted (sparger airflow blocked) glucose-excess chemostat of E. coli MC4100; not a strictly reduced/anaerobic medium -- no cysteine/sulfide reductant is added and no defined gas mix is used, so 'anaerobic' here means O2-limited/fermentative. The base is Miller (1972) Minimal Medium A (confirmed as the Ferenci-lab basal salts medium in Tweeddale et al. 1998 and used for this paper's plates), plus FeSO4/citrate and thiamine, supplemented with 0.08% glucose.
